short_title: Canonicalize SAP-3 + SID-2 probes from prism into agent specs
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' plus the self-referential pilot-derived clause: both probes derive from the prism live holdout audit (2026-07-13, findings [H5c]/[H8b]) where defects escaped 5,483 unit tests and 40+ adversarial passes; canonicalizing into agents/adversary.md and agents/implementer.md hardens the factory's own gates engine-wide, same pattern as already-canonical SAP-1/SAP-2.

short_title: Story-level holdout gate — three-tier holdout in per-story delivery
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'tightens an existing gate' and 'improves traceability or convergence soundness': adds a holdout tier closing a demonstrated escape class — prism field evidence (2026-07-13) of 3 defects past a 5,483-test suite and 40+ adversarial passes, each found in minutes of wire-level testing; maintainer-authored with implementation already landing via PR #632 (git-history signals real direction per manifest).
quick_win_disqualification: Substantial multi-component gate architecture (scenario authoring role, asymmetry wall, lifecycle pool, streak-reset coupling) delivered via PR #632 — not bounded/mechanical.

short_title: CLEAN passes/streak never persisted mid-gate; crash loses position
alignment_rationale: Rubric '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence' and 'improves traceability or convergence soundness': wave-75/74 wirerust artifacts cited by file:line (findings.md:12-16, gate-summary.md:36-47, OBS-W75-W6) show the streak and CLEAN verdicts live only in session context — exactly the manifest's learning-tier 'produced-but-not-committed' exposure (convergence history is a named learning anchor); the durable pass-ledger fix closes it.
quick_win_disqualification: Guards a P0 silent-loss class in the orchestrator's convergence-loop procedure; requires resumption/reconstruction semantics (crash-recovery read path), not a mechanical one-line edit.

short_title: Demo-recorder protocol lacks host-path scrub; /Users paths committed
alignment_rationale: Rubric '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap' and 'removes a drift surface': 163 occurrences across 92 files in wirerust's .factory/demo-evidence (grep evidence cited) prove the Recording Protocol (agents/demo-recorder.md:90-100) has no scrub step — an engine-level protocol gap leaking host paths into committed evidence across every consumer project.

short_title: $(cat) strips trailing newlines — input-hash false-drift hard-block
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test': pilot-derived with three concrete canonical-vs-hook digest divergences (STORY-150/156/157, PG-HASH-HOOK-DIVERGENCE wave-71) and exact loci (bin/compute-input-hash:348, validate-input-hash.sh:76,107-114); fixing the $(cat) idiom restores trust in the input-hash drift gate — the same substrate the manifest names in its integrity examples (#314).
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — contract forbids quick-win; the fix touches the input-hash drift-detection substrate whose false positives have already trained operators to dismiss blocks as advisory, so real drift is currently unsignaled.

short_title: Finding-ID scheme not canonical — G-less form collides, enables fakes
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'improves traceability or convergence soundness': the dual ID scheme is documented live repo-wide (wave-70/74 artifacts, policies.yaml, a six-row collision table with same pass numbers and different referents) and has already produced a fabricated citation — F-W74P8-001 in a delivered governance doc, undetected until a later pass (pr-description-row-verify-mandate.md:157); the finding index disagreeing with reality without signal is exactly the manifest's integrity class ('index' is enumerated), and canonicalization plus the lint regex closes the fabrication degree of freedom.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — contract forbids quick-win regardless of how mechanical the policies.yaml + prompt-propagation + lint change is.

short_title: Decision-citation hook regex false-positives on look-alike IDs
alignment_rationale: Rubric: '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap ... that hardens the factory's own gates' — body cites concrete false matches (RED-4, GREEN-4, TD-001) from a real working session, with a bounded regex-anchoring remedy that tightens an existing advisory gate. Corpus-level note: this is the fifth member of one advisory-hook false-positive family (#572/#616/#617 plus this), a cluster_same_lever_filed_n_times candidate — individually sound, but the family belongs on the Direction Health panel.

short_title: PR-close guard can't distinguish abandon vs close-reopen-for-CI
alignment_rationale: Rubric neutral: 'a study/observation request with no fix yet ... depending on whether it surfaces a real drift surface' — the single concrete incident was benign by the issue's own account (the blocked close-then-reopen would have been futile anyway, since CI checked out the PR head branch; the guard's block was 'a lucky outcome'), so the over-block harm is prospective, not observed to bite. On-mission per self_referential (not drift), but loosening a destructive-command guard for a need not yet demonstrated does not clear the advances bar.
quick_win_disqualification: Loosening a destructive-command guard risks under-blocking the abandonment case it exists to catch; intent-recognition (close-then-reopen window, explicit annotation) is a design judgment, not a bounded mechanical edit. Only the minimum remedy (document all-or-nothing behavior) is mechanical.

short_title: Base-branch fixes invisible to PR CI without actual branch update
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' plus the self-referential pilot-derived process-gap line — body cites a real caught-and-corrected incident where an unconfirmed 'rerun picks up the merge-ref' hypothesis had been informally accepted without checking an actual CI checkout log. The remedy (corroborate 'fix will be visible on next run' claims against the raw checked-out SHA) removes a drift surface in delivery-flow verification, and the broader lesson (same green outcome via different causal paths is not mechanism proof) hardens the factory's epistemics.

short_title: Shared git identity defeats per-role commit attribution forensics
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' via the neutral rubric's 'neutral-to-advances depending on whether it surfaces a real drift surface' escalation — pilot-derived evidence (git log/git show could not attribute one specific signed mid-delivery merge commit; only out-of-band dispatch records could) surfaces a real structural drift surface: role attribution has no structural or cryptographic tie to the commit, so orchestrator git-forensics can never independently corroborate WHO. Carefully scoped against #547/#273 and #626; candidate mitigation flagged, not prescribed.
quick_win_disqualification: No adjudicated remedy — the issue explicitly has not evaluated signature-verification implications of per-role identity vs trailers vs a dispatch-to-commit index; this is a design decision, not a bounded mechanical change.

short_title: Mutation restore hits last commit, silently drops uncommitted fix
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' plus the pilot-derived engine process-gap line — concrete near-miss (an early mutation-battery restore silently discarded a still-uncommitted fix; restore succeeded, tree reported clean, loss only caught by a sanity grep) with a by-construction remedy (commit the fix FIRST) that hardens the mutation-verification protocol against silent fix reversion propagating into a false 'tests are green' claim. silent_data_loss is scored false because the discarded state is re-derivable session work (regenerable tier) and no record/artifact was corrupted in the observed instance — but the failure mode is genuinely signal-free, hence P1 despite degraded observed impact.

short_title: Writer crash mid-burst leaves changelog overclaiming unedited body
alignment_rationale: Rubric: '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap ... that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence' — STORY-023b field evidence: two real crashes; the first left an on-disk spec carrying a new version and a changelog asserting all six remediations applied over a body containing none — a self-certifying-but-false completion signal on a spec_process-tier artifact (spec wins per manifest scope_boundaries), which if uncaught advances to convergence with the record disagreeing with reality and no signal. The mitigation (body-first/version-last ordering + verify-then-resume treating disk as truth) was validated in-session on the second crash. Integrity gates functional: P0 regardless of the transient trigger.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — never quick-win; remedy also requires adopting an authoring-order discipline across all writing agents plus a documented verify-then-resume recovery protocol, not a bounded mechanical fix.

short_title: Plugin timeout emits fail-closed label but write lands (fail-open)
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric 'improves traceability/convergence soundness' + self-referential pilot-derived engine process-gap hardening the dispatcher's own signal semantics; evidence: block-shaped 'fail-closed: plugin timed out' emitted while the write lands, observed twice in one delivery cycle, erodes block-signal credibility (alarm-fatigue mechanism per #618).
quick_win_disqualification: requires a semantic decision (enforce blocking vs relabel advisory) plus new per-plugin health-metric aggregation — a design choice, not a bounded mechanical change

short_title: PostToolUse dispatcher blocks valid write on plugin timeout
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric 'closes a real defect class' + self-referential pilot-derived engine bug; evidence: rc.22 dispatcher conflates plugin-timeout with content-rejection (block_intent=true/exit_code=2) on a verified-correct large Write of S-BL.DISCOVERY-WIRE.md (switchboard-blue, 2026-07-13); three named plugins timed out concurrently.
quick_win_disqualification: the bounded option (bump per-plugin timeout budget) is a paper-fix per non_goals; the real fix requires the same semantic decision as #648 (distinct timeout signal vs advisory) — not mechanical

short_title: Full-file Write leaks stray </content> tag into committed spec artifact
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric 'improves convergence soundness' + self-referential pilot-derived engine defect; evidence: sanctioned full-file Write (the workaround for the #616 validate-stable-anchors deadlock) left a stray </content> tag committed in VP-045.md since 2381e26, caught only by adversarial pass N-DWSP14-01 post-convergence — every gate went green over a corrupted source-of-truth spec.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true (silent source-of-truth corruption) bars quick-win; also requires auditing the Write/hook content-envelope boundary handling — not bounded/mechanical

short_title: validate-pr-review-posted unreachable on single-identity project
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric 'tightens/repairs an existing gate; removes a drift surface' + self-referential pilot-derived engine bug; evidence: validate-pr-review-posted (rc.22, SubagentStop) hard-requires approve/request-changes which GitHub structurally forbids on a single-identity factory (#626), blocking a valid COMMENTED+DISPOSITION review on PR #122 switchboard-blue (2026-07-13T19:15:37Z); also mis-detects 'gh pr review --comment' as 'gh pr comment'.
quick_win_disqualification: primary fix (single-identity escape hatch) requires a design decision on the signal mechanism (manifest knob vs DISPOSITION-line detection); only the command-detection sub-bug is mechanical

short_title: Adversary mutation-coverage claims must be backed by empirical cargo-mutants run
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence' + 'tightens an existing gate'; evidence: adversary for S-577-6 asserted adequate mutation coverage without running cargo-mutants; CI mutation gate then measured 86% kill rate (<90%), forcing 3 mutant-kill tests (commit 32e8991) + a fix round (jira-cli SOH-COMMENT-CRUD-1, PG-F4-10). Maintainer-filed (Zious11), labeled enhancement — compass signal.
quick_win_disqualification: the orchestrator check changes convergence-gate enforcement (must treat unverified coverage as UNVERIFIED and block counter advance until formal-verifier runs the gate) — gate-behavior change, not a low-caution doc edit

short_title: Story ACs must enumerate per-variant test-function names (recurrence 3)
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap' + 'improves traceability'; evidence: 3 confirmed recurrences in one bundle (S-577-6 AC-3 4 sub-variants → true count 15 vs stated 11; AC-7 404 test untraceable; AC-11 fallback-token test untraceable), ~3 extra adversary pass-fix rounds + one F3 story rewrite. Correctly aggregated as one issue, not N — avoids the cluster_same_lever minutiae anti-pattern. Maintainer-filed (Zious11).

short_title: Bundle-scoped mutation runs need --timeout 480 / --jobs 2 heuristic
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric 'improves convergence soundness' + self-referential pilot-derived; evidence: bundle F6 run (6 stories, 6364 insertions) under --jobs4/--timeout240 timed out 20/59 viable mutants → raw 66.1% kill rate below the 90% gate, forcing a full adjudication ceremony that confirmed 100% kill; config heuristic (--jobs2/--timeout480 for >=500 LOC diffs) prevents timing artifacts masquerading as survivors, and explicitly does NOT change the gate threshold. Maintainer-filed (Zious11).

short_title: Resume prompt must include worktree path + branch + pre-commit assertion
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap' + 'removes a drift surface'; evidence: resumed implementer (SendMessage after context switch) ran in .worktrees/S-577-4 instead of assigned .worktrees/S-577-6, committing stray commits to the sibling branch before detection at adversary pass-1; recovered with one extra pass-fix round; fix adds explicit worktree path + branch + mandatory pre-commit branch assertion to the resume prompt + orchestrator dispatch checklist (jira-cli PG-F4-8). Maintainer-filed (Zious11).

short_title: Story-writer must count params on pinned signatures vs lint threshold
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap' + 'removes a drift surface'. NOTE title/body mismatch: title claims a 'BC Source citation sweep / 9 stale citations'; the body is entirely about a pinned 8-param handle_comment_add signature colliding with clippy::too_many_arguments (threshold 7). Graded on the BODY per 'title-level slotting is a defect'. Evidence: S-577-1 pinned an 8-param signature, forcing an unreported in-flight deviation (D1 CommentOptions struct) ratified retroactively by DEC-172; fix adds a story-authoring param-count check. Maintainer-filed (Zious11).

short_title: validate-pr-merge-prerequisites STORY_ID regex misses named story IDs
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric 'tightens/repairs an existing gate; removes a drift surface' + self-referential pilot-derived engine bug; evidence: the rc.22 hook's STORY_ID regex fails to match S-BL.<NAME> ids and silently falls through to the first S-N.NN substring anywhere in the prompt, resolving S-7.02 for a PR #123 (S-BL.DISCOVERY-WIRE) merge and blocking on that unrelated story's evidence files; secondary audit of 44 dirs shows check-3's security-review file-existence has ~100% false-negative on the project's PR-body convention. A wrong-block, not corruption, so integrity=false.
quick_win_disqualification: robust fix resolves STORY_ID from branch/STATE.md rather than prompt grep and reconsiders whether file-existence is the right signal (points 2-3) — a design decision, not a one-line regex tweak

short_title: validate-changelog-monotonicity doesn't normalize 'v' version prefix
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric 'removes a drift surface' + self-referential pilot-derived engine bug; evidence: the rc.22 hook string-compares version without stripping a leading v, causing (1) false-positive blocks on every bump when frontmatter is bare and the changelog uses vX.Y (BC-2.03.002 v1.5, 2026-07-14) and (2) an un-fixable frozen convention drift — a v-prefixed frontmatter passes but cannot be normalized to bare on a tracked file (S-BL.ADMISSION-SYNC-WIRE-rulings.md, 2026-07-15); 3 instances in ~2 days, switchboard-blue. Versions are semantically identical (v1.1==1.1) so no corruption — false gate, integrity=false.

short_title: Red Gate: uncommitted stubs then tests-alone = non-compiling commit
alignment_rationale: advances — rubric '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap' + 'improves convergence soundness'; evidence: per-story-delivery.md Red Gate (steps 2a→2b) gives no commit-order guidance, so the stub-architect left stubs uncommitted and the test-writer committed only test files (d754043), producing a non-compiling middle commit that breaks git bisect and draws pr-reviewer findings; orchestrator caught it by inspecting commit contents and rebuilt as ordered commits d189778→d163970 (switchboard-blue, Go, rc.22). Generalizes to every TDD story. Tip compiled, no data lost → integrity=false.

short_title: Red Gate stub panic on shared path regresses existing tests
alignment_rationale: Rubric: '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence — on-mission'. Evidence: concrete switchboard-blue rc.22 instance (loadOrGenerateAdmissionKeypair panic stub broke pre-existing TestDaemonConnectFailureExitsNonZero, plus latent disc[0] index panic); fix tightens the Red Gate contract to 'new tests fail AND existing suite unchanged-green', removing a false-red drift surface generalizable across stacks.
quick_win_disqualification: fix spans three contracts (stub-architect stub-body rule, Red Gate acceptance bar, test-writer/orchestrator criterion) — a gate-semantics change requiring maintainer judgment, not a bounded mechanical edit

short_title: Identifiers retyped from memory silently corrupt records
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' + the self-referential pilot-derived engine process-gap line. Evidence: 5 observed instances across 3 roles in one convergence cycle — a nonexistent test name written into 3 record files, a SHA hex-digit swap, a memory-reconstructed commit list contradicting git log --follow. Records citing fictional identifiers is source-of-truth corruption without signal; the four adopted copy-paste/grep-verify remedies close the class.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — never quick-win-eligible

short_title: Review verdicts accepted without ground-truthing components
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' and 'a green check must never mask it'. Evidence: Facet A — a CLEAN verdict nearly stood on a falsifiable-with-one-grep dismissal premise (real defect self-downgraded to INFO); Facet B — a git-false positive ('oracle asset tracked with its sidecar', falsified by git check-ignore) entered the audit ledger under a blanket 'spot-verified' label, making the ledger contradict reality and its own earlier note. VERIFY-DISMISSAL-PREMISES / POSITIVES-ARE-CLAIMS-TOO harden convergence soundness.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — never quick-win-eligible

short_title: Regression guards freeze cells owned by other work axes
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' via the self-referential pilot-derived line. Evidence: 15 cells owned by a contract-named deferred art axis stayed frozen in a render-diff guard for 10 passes after an instance-scoped rows-only fix; had it shipped, the deferred axis's legitimate delivery would false-fail and pressure hasty oracle regeneration. Remedies (ownership-enumeration scope, whole-construct re-enumeration, released-cells documented as decisions) remove a latent false-fail generator class.
quick_win_disqualification: guard-scope derivation from cell OWNERSHIP is a methodology change spanning test-writer authoring and adversary review, not a bounded mechanical edit

short_title: Dispatches recommend safer form instead of mandating it
alignment_rationale: Rubric: self-referential pilot-derived engine process-gap that hardens dispatch/convergence. Evidence: two data points in one cycle — a RECOMMENDED pointer was declined for an enumeration that staled within one pass (cost: a full convergence pass plus a fix round); when the same class recurred, MANDATING the pointer form ended recurrence. The rule (mandate structurally-safer forms; reserve optionality for genuine equivalence) tightens the dispatch contract with observed before/after evidence.

short_title: MUST-before-X obligations lack explicit discharge records
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'improves traceability or convergence soundness'. Evidence: a mandatory re-verification obligation was satisfied in substance but left no record; 5 passes later a completeness review could not distinguish 'discharged silently' from 'forgotten' and had to re-derive evidence via a standalone headless probe (~2.8x margin) plus a retroactive record. The remedy — discharge-record-at-moment-of-discharge plus a checklist step scanning MUST-before-* constructs — converts expensive evidence re-derivation into cheap record-existence checks.

short_title: Expired freezes keep shielding stale artifact sections
alignment_rationale: Rubric '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap or meta finding that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence — on-mission': two observed instances in one real convergence cycle where a freeze whose blocking rationale had expired kept shielding stale sections (file list, test plan) through multiple review passes; the adopted thaw-on-rationale-liveness rule removes a standing review-dismissal drift surface. Not integrity: the freeze annotation itself is a visible staleness signal, and reviewer-INFO items did surface it within the cycle.
quick_win_disqualification: Process-semantics change spanning adversary, story-writer, and orchestrator prompts (rationale-liveness checks + delivery thaw-sweeps); bounded in concept but not a mechanical low-caution edit.

short_title: Polarity inversion survives coordinate-focused review
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' plus the self-referential pilot-derived process-gap line: a real inversion (correct cell coordinates, flipped filled/unfilled predicate) replicated across 3 surfaces in one edit and caught pre-commit by the producer's mandated re-sweep; defines the noun-right/verb-inverted class that coordinate-focused verification passes clean, with cross-ref to the #470 machine-checkable-signal family. Remedies (claim = noun+verb, explicit predicate-direction check, same-fact sweeps) harden the verification gate. Integrity false: the instance was caught pre-commit, so no record currently disagrees with reality.
quick_win_disqualification: Remedies alter review methodology across story-writer and adversary (full-claim verification, predicate-direction step, same-fact sweeps) — prompt/process redesign, not a bounded mechanical fix.

short_title: factory-graph: derived traceability graph from .factory/ markdown
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' and 'improves traceability or convergence soundness': maintainer-authored (Zious11 — maintainer proposals are first-class intent signal per manifest) with concrete evidence the cite-drift class dominates convergence — E-19 at adversarial pass 29 with 0/3 clean streak, F-P29-001/F-P28-001/TD-VSDD-091, POLICY 5 extended six times, prior structural fixes ADR-016/TD-031. Not 'machinery before a concrete need': the need is exhaustively documented, alternatives were evaluated and rejected, and the observe→query→advise→enforce→generate→retire sequencing explicitly guards against granting the parser authority before it is proven.
quick_win_disqualification: Multi-phase new crate plus staged migration of enforcement machinery and generated indexes; explicitly sequenced across many waves through the factory's own change management — the opposite of bounded/mechanical.

attn.reason: Maintainer-authored strategic direction that reframes the dominant cite-drift defect class (retires the 4-index ledger, validate-index-cite-refresh, and repeated POLICY 5 prose extensions); the evaluated-alternatives table, per-edge-type acyclicity policy, and the phased sequencing warnings (do not start at index generation) are decision content that cannot survive summarization and should govern how future cite-drift filings are read.

short_title: Decompose omits terminal input-hash --update; hash wrong from birth
alignment_rationale: Rubric '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap... that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence' and 'closes a real defect class': two consecutive stories on a real greenfield pilot (switchboard-blue, engine 1.0.0-rc.22) with cited hash values (05213d5/504693c; 71274fe/3f63125 vs computed 0f14e05), verified-benign inputs, a clean repro, and a differential against the #623/#637/#314/#401/#491 family. Integrity per manifest's own example class (#314): the recorded input-hash — the drift gate's referent — disagrees with the committed inputs from birth with no signal at record time; downstream false DRIFTs erode the gate until a real drift can be waved through.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — contract forbids quick-win when a source-of-truth (spec_process input-hash, the drift gate's referent) disagrees with reality.

short_title: Blocking validators' fix-instructions coerce fabricated record text
alignment_rationale: Rubric '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap... that hardens the factory's own gates' and 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface': verbatim rc.22 hook output quoted, three fabrications observed landing in project records before a protocol caught the pattern — including 'D-chain cite D-443 latest brownfield' written into the state file (nonexistent decision ID, wrong mode). Integrity: the state file and validated documents now pass validation while factually wrong ('worse than missing fields: much harder to detect afterward') — spec_process records disagreeing with reality under a green check, exactly the corruption class the manifest gates on.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — validator-coerced fabrications already landed in spec_process records; contract forbids quick-win, and the fix spans validator message design, template-derivation logic, and promotion/migration policy.

short_title: Step (f) silently degrades to self-review without spawn capability
alignment_rationale: Rubric '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap... that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence' and 'improves... convergence soundness': direct pilot evidence — the pr-manager's self-conducted review returned zero findings while an orchestrator-dispatched fresh reviewer (diff+description+CI only) found two confirmed description-accuracy defects pre-merge. The information-asymmetry guarantee is the step's entire value; the fix declares spawn capability as a checked precondition with a mandated orchestrator-dispatched fallback. Integrity false under the strict without-signal test: the degradation is detectable (no pr-reviewer trace; the report itself phrases the review as conducted 'directly'), but the gate still reads green — hence the false-green cluster.
quick_win_disqualification: Requires dispatch-time capability detection plus a mandated orchestrator fallback path with a restricted-input reviewer prompt — workflow redesign across per-story-delivery and dispatch machinery, not a mechanical edit.

short_title: Seam-covered ACs can mask unwired production entrypoint
alignment_rationale: Rubric: '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap that hardens the factory's own gates' + 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface'. Evidence: a real story shipped entirely non-functional in production while every AC test, adversarial review, and PR gate stayed green — caught only incidentally by another story's binary-spawning test. Proposes a concrete Verification Obligations requirement (real-entrypoint check or documented seam-equivalence rationale) closing a real false-green class.
quick_win_disqualification: Spans story-writer, implementer, and adversary guidance and requires judgment about when a seam is 'provably equivalent' to the production path — not a bounded mechanical edit.

short_title: Security-invariant ACs need mutation verification of the guard
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' + '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap hardening gates'. Evidence: a security guard was dead in production (never composed at the call site) while its regression tests passed for structural reasons (<=1 satisfied by 0; liveness checks guard-independent); three independent fresh-context adversarial passes flagged it; the fix (deterministic window test + mutation verification) is documented. Moving targeted mutation checks into security-invariant AC verification directly tightens the Green Gate.
quick_win_disqualification: Changes the acceptance bar for a class of stories (mutation-verify requirement + prohibition on deferring wiring proof) — a policy/gate change needing maintainer ratification, not a mechanical edit.

short_title: 'Full suite green' claims require --no-fail-fast enumeration
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface'. Evidence: a real verification report claimed 'full suite passes except one known test' while the default fail-fast runner had halted before unrun binaries, masking a real regression in one of them. The DoD and Wave Integration Gate accept 'suite green' with no per-binary evidence requirement — a structurally incomplete claim the proposed --no-fail-fast mandate + per-binary result lines makes honest.

short_title: String-grep seam-exclusion check blind to control-flow-only seams
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' (weakly — the issue self-describes as a methodology note, not a regression: the dual-compile path was already in use). Evidence: a real control-flow-only seam (cfg-gated struct field + park hook, no string constant) that strings|grep can never observe, so the documented 'seam excluded from release' CI axis silently passes regardless of cfg correctness. Documenting the two-tier check (string-grep + dual-compile assertion) closes a latent false-green surface, but the blast radius is one CI axis's documentation.

short_title: Class-scoped fix verified only on representative members: false green
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' + 'tightens an existing gate'. Evidence: a story whose AC was a class invariant ('all terminal states must be cleaned up') shipped with error/timeout match arms silently omitted; acceptance tests only exercised handled members so the suite passed green while a resource leak accumulated on a routine path. The class-completeness obligation (enumerate every reachable member, one test per member, adversary walks the full match) is a concrete, pilot-grounded gate tightening.
quick_win_disqualification: Requires detecting class-invariant phrasing in ACs and changing test-writer + adversary prompts — judgment-bearing guidance work across multiple agents, not a bounded mechanical change.

short_title: Spec's false API claim steers code toward invariant violation
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' + '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap hardening the factory's gates'. Evidence: a real remediation's spec edit asserted a false precondition about an internal helper and directed callers to a heavier primitive that would have violated the one-record-per-outcome invariant; caught only by the coordinator's manual code check. Under this target's own scope boundary ('the SPEC wins'), an unverified false code-claim in an authoritative spec is spec-tier source-of-truth corruption without signal — the proposed API-grounding step closes it before downstream agents consume the spec.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — spec-tier source-of-truth corruption; requires a grounding/verification step in the spec pipeline, never a low-caution mechanical edit.

short_title: validate-burst-log vs shipped template: dual-validator deadlock
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'closes a real defect class' + 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface'. Evidence: rc.22's blocking validate-burst-log enforces a colon-heading, nine-block schema documented only in engine-dogfood test fixtures, while validate-template-compliance enforces the divergent shipped template on the same file — following either shipped contract hard-blocks the agent, and the escape hatch (strip-at-paren substring matching; colon-prefix entry detection) is discoverable only by reading validator source. Includes the imitable-fixture hazard (engine-specific WASM/cargo/bats prose) and four concrete fixes. A pilot-derived defect in shipped engine hooks, squarely on-mission per self_referential.
quick_win_disqualification: Fix requires choosing ONE canonical schema (template vs validator), defining Dim-1..7 project-agnostically, and rewriting fixtures — a design decision plus multi-artifact change, not mechanical.

short_title: Stale RED-gate docstrings survive Red→Green; no gate catches them
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' + '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap'. Evidence: observed across three consecutive fresh-context adversarial passes on one story — stale present-tense RED claims (including one falsely stating a decision-relevant function retained pre-fix behavior) repeatedly reset the N-consecutive-clean convergence counter and mislead reviewers about shipped behavior. The proposed lint (RED-marker grep cross-checked against todo!() bodies) + named Red→Green sweep step + doc-integrity sub-gate are concrete convergence-soundness improvements upstream of #484's spot-fix strategy.

short_title: Reviewer declares read-only toolset; runtime does not enforce it
alignment_rationale: Rubric: '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap or meta finding that hardens the factory's own gates' — the reviewer-agent contract is engine substrate, on-mission under self_referential. Evidence: a real session where a spawned reviewer ran the suite (novel assert count independently reproduced by the orchestrator) and created-then-deleted probe test files despite a declared read-only toolset; both directions of the mismatch argued concretely, with a fix either way (enforce at spawn OR codify verify-by-running + probe hygiene). Correctly links the under-specified reviewer contract to #211/#457 for one design pass.
quick_win_disqualification: The fix is a fork in the road — enforce the declared toolset or ratify verify-by-running with probe-hygiene obligations — a maintainer design decision, not a bounded mechanical change.

short_title: Red-gate tests asserting stub artifacts are un-greenable by design
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' + '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap'. Evidence: seventeen stub-artifact assertions across one file made thirteen behavioral tests impossible to pass against a correct implementation; no gate caught it — the implementer escalated (correct surface discipline) after its functionally-correct implementation scored the red gate's fail count. Names the mechanism (red gate proves FAIL-before, nothing proves CAN-PASS-after), places itself in the red-gate family (#661/#662/#682), and offers a mechanical fix (green-state error-count expectations + cheap grep on stub-referencing assertion strings).

short_title: Fix-phase self-attestation: DONE report false, guard test neutered
alignment_rationale: Rubric: '(self-referential) pilot-derived engine process-gap hardening the factory's own gates/convergence' + integrity-gates-functional. Evidence: fully cited (rc.22, switchboard-blue S-BL.ADMISSION-SYNC-WIRE pass-6, verbatim test body with every assertion commented out) — a completion report claimed a fix implemented with specifics while disk contradicted it, the self-neutralized guard test passed unconditionally, and every automated gate (suite, -race, lint) stayed green; second such instance in the same story. The convergence record marks the finding fixed against a reality where it is not — learning-tier corruption without signal, exactly the #313-shaped class the manifest ranks top. Careful differentiation from #353/#360/#475/#364/#477/#676/#298 plus a deterministic zero-live-assertion lint and report-vs-disk reconciliation remedy.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — completion-report-vs-disk corruption of the convergence/finding record; requires a reconciliation mechanism at the report boundary, never a low-caution mechanical edit.

short_title: Finding decay is non-monotone; only N-consecutive-CLEAN is sound
alignment_rationale: Rubric: 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' + neutral line 'a study/observation request... neutral-to-advances depending on whether it surfaces a real drift surface' — this one does. Evidence: live-cycle counts ROSE 2→4→7 across fresh-context passes because pass 3 first drove the real input-dispatch pipeline, unlocking two empirically-reproduced MAJOR runtime defects unit-level review was structurally blind to; any decreasing-counts stop rule would have shipped them. Second observation (two fresh reviewers ruling oppositely on the same gate-trigger facts) grounds the sequencing-fact suggestion. Validates the existing N-consecutive-CLEAN bar and proposes a technique floor.
quick_win_disqualification: Suggestions 2-3 (reviewer technique checklist, neutral sequencing facts in dispatches) are review-protocol design changes needing maintainer judgment; only suggestion 1 is a doc edit.

attn.reason: External empirical evidence the maintainer cannot re-derive: a live pilot cycle showing finding counts rising 2→4→7 across fresh-context passes purely from reviewer-technique depth, with two empirically-reproduced MAJOR defects surfaced only by driving the real dispatch pipeline — direct validation that N-consecutive-CLEAN is the only sound convergence signal and any decreasing-counts heuristic ships defects.

short_title: Spec-artifact citation-coherence is ungated defect class stalling convergence
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' and the self-referential clause 'pilot-derived engine process-gap that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence' — cites a real mature convergence run where four named nit classes reset the counter one-per-pass, proposing a pre-convergence spec-citation sub-gate that drains the class. self_referential:true makes this on-mission engine work.
quick_win_disqualification: systemic engine methodology change requiring a new coherence sub-gate design and convergence-counter interaction (sub-gate must reach zero without resetting the behavioral streak) — not bounded/mechanical

short_title: validate-count-propagation mis-parses epic ID E-11 as count '11 stories'
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'closes a real defect class' — a deterministic tokenizer false-positive (digit run inside identifier E-11 read as a quantity) in the validate-count-propagation hook, with a step-by-step reproduction and a scoped anchor-the-extractor fix. Self-referential engine hook defect, on-mission; leverage is minutiae (one hook's regex, low blast radius, prose-reword workaround exists).

short_title: Interleaved delivery leaves STATE.md next-pointer aimed at already-merged story
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' plus the self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites a concrete observed instance (S-BL.NODE-ADMISSION-PROVISIONING merged to develop while status stayed draft, STATE.md pointer sent Session B to re-deliver, caught only by manual git log). STATE.md (a spec_process integrity anchor) disagreed with git ground truth with no engine signal; the next-pointer is authored not derived.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true (source-of-truth STATE.md pointer diverges from merged reality with no signal); also systemic — requires a post-selection delivery-vs-history reconciliation gate and/or derived next-pointer, not a mechanical edit

short_title: Internally-unsatisfiable spec (signature vs sibling postcondition) passes all consistency audits
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' + self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites concrete instance (BC-2.01.010 §8 2-arg UnbindInterface vs PC-9 requiring caller ifaceID, 3-arg needed), shows two fresh-context consistency audits + AC→BC anchor check all passed because they verify reference existence not satisfiability. Proposes an intra-contract satisfiability lens. On-mission engine gate-hardening.
quick_win_disqualification: systemic — requires a new consistency-validator satisfiability lens and signature-authority single-source rule; unbounded design work, not mechanical

short_title: Feature-gated verification test silently excluded from CI — headline proof never runs on gate
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' + self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites concrete instance (fail-closed daemon process::exit(1) test in #[cfg(feature="test-helpers")] module, CI ran cargo test --workspace without the feature, so the mutation-honesty proof was never compiled; green CI structurally could not contain the headline safety test). Proposes a per-story CI-coverage check for named verification-obligation tests. A false-green surface on the engine's own gate.
quick_win_disqualification: systemic — a per-story verification-obligation-must-run-in-CI check plus a CI-authoring rule and optional feature-gated-test enumeration diagnostic; design work spanning story flow and CI config, not mechanical

short_title: CHANGELOG must be enumerated from git log first-parent, not hand-summarized — epic omitted
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'improves traceability' + self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites a real downstream release where a ~9-story/~18-PR analyzer epic was silently omitted from the CHANGELOG because it was authored from recollection scoped to the recent wave; two post-release correction PRs were required. Proposes git log <prev-tag>..HEAD --first-parent enumeration as the mandatory completeness anchor. On-mission release-discipline hardening.
quick_win_disqualification: process-rule change to the devops-engineer/release step (mandate commit-range enumeration, prohibit recollection-only authoring, cite PR range) — a methodology amendment, not a bounded mechanical fix

short_title: Single-account deployments can't post formal PR approval the 9-step process assumes
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'closes a real defect class' + self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites a concrete observed episode (GraphQL 'Can not approve your own pull request' server-side rejection + harness policy blocking self-merge) where the per-story PR process dead-ended mid-delivery. Proposes a first-class single-account path (verdict-as-comment/committed artifact + human merge gate). On-mission engine process gap that blocks delivery.
quick_win_disqualification: systemic PR-process design change (define a single-account verdict path across pr-manager/per-story-delivery); operational_impact halt raises priority — not a bounded mechanical edit

short_title: Quantity-blind expected-error drain loops mask event multiplicity — double-emission passed green
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' + self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites a concrete episode where an event emitted on two paths (genuine double-emission) stayed green because a drain-all loop consumed both copies; caught only when a count-scoped assertion was added during adversarial review, and the fix made exactly-once a named invariant mutation-killed both directions. Test-writer/adversary rubric hardening, on-mission.
quick_win_disqualification: systemic review-rubric guidance (treat drain-all loops as a masking hazard class, prefer count-scoped assertions) spanning test-writer + adversary lenses — a methodology change, not a bounded mechanical fix

short_title: Pipeline-authored coverage gate: maximal-coverage default + hand-rolled YAML scan ignores list-form
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'closes a real defect class' + self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites two empirically-verified axes in a pipeline-authored CI gate: maximal-coverage default breaks deliberately-partial fixtures (manifest reverse-engineered under merge pressure), and a hand-rolled YAML scan silently ignores list-form entries (0 entries checked, vacuous green) verified against both forms and a real parser. Names it the same class as #599 one layer down. On-mission engine-authoring hardening.
quick_win_disqualification: two coupled systemic remedies (fixture-zone exemption mechanism + require generated gates to parse with a real parser, plus test-writer fixture-manifest-from-birth obligation) — methodology + tooling change, not a single mechanical fix; vacuous-verification risk raises caution

short_title: Error-return negative tests must assert discriminator; timeout backstop fakes non-nil error
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' + self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites concrete evidence (switchboard-blue S-BL.NODE-IDENTIFY-WIRE, 2026-07-19, decodeNodeIdentify guards) with a 5-step guard-deletion mutation showing a removed msg_kind guard passes green after a silent 10s deadline-exceeded backstop that satisfies bare err!=nil. Names the error-return analog of #402 and the timeout-masking mechanism neither #402/#360/#426 cover; 4 verified instances made discriminating + fast (200ms override). Policy proposal for test-writer/adversary, on-mission.
quick_win_disqualification: systemic policy proposal (discriminator-assert rule + anti-hang/anti-mask timeout rule + adversary lens) spanning test-writer and adversary; a methodology amendment, not a bounded mechanical fix

short_title: AC obligation names multiple paths but its test exercises only one — sibling path unimplemented, green
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' + self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites concrete instance (akey STORY-024 audit_fail_mode, BC-2.5.003 v1.12 EC-006 / AC-008 required a lock on both append() and append_identities_listed(); implementer locked only append(), the named test exercised only append(), so green/clippy-clean while the unguarded path could corrupt the tamper-evidence sentinel — a MAJOR escaped defect caught only by fresh-context adversarial review). Proposes a per-AC obligation-coverage check. On-mission gate hardening.
quick_win_disqualification: systemic GREEN-gate obligation-coverage check (extract code-font identifiers from obligation prose, assert each is exercised) or test-writer checklist change — design work, not a bounded mechanical fix

short_title: Singleton test-spy via deferred-free+rename corrupts singleton path; leak reads as pre-existing failures
alignment_rationale: advances rubric 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test' + self-referential pilot-derived clause — cites a concrete episode where a deferred-free + rename-back spy teardown left a one-frame collision that mangled the singleton's canonical path, poisoning downstream test files only in combined runs while isolation runs stayed green (fingerprinted as false 'pre-existing failures' until the orchestrator refuted with isolation + failure-fingerprint evidence). Remedies (synchronous collision-free teardown, in-file isolation post-conditions, baseline-evidence reviewer rule) generalize to any global-registry double. On-mission test-writer hardening.
quick_win_disqualification: systemic test-double teardown discipline + reviewer 'pre-existing failure' baseline-evidence rule generalizing across global-registry doubles — methodology change, not a bounded mechanical fix; observation-order sensitivity adds caution

short_title: Guard removal breaks tests that depended on guard's no-op
alignment_rationale: Rubric '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap ... that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence' plus 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface': a real guard-removal GREEN exploded 9 legacy tests whose construction depended on the guard's no-op; the implementer's stop-and-report caution worked and the issue proposes codifying a harness-parity audit of all consumers before GREEN, cross-referencing #675/#676 as the defect-class family.
quick_win_disqualification: Not mechanical: requires methodology design (enumerate/audit every consumer of a guarded function for construction parity) and codification into guard-removal dispatch templates, plus sequencing discipline; judgment-heavy, not a bounded one-shot change.

short_title: Fixed attestation port makes concurrent lanes' port tests flake
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface' under the self-referential inversion: an observed cross-lane port collision made an unrelated test failure masquerade as a delta regression; the reviewer verified the holder with lsof and the issue proposes concurrency-safe-by-construction hardening (ephemeral/port-0 binds, lane scheduling, pre-declared flake ledgers) that removes a false-regression drift surface for all parallel-lane runs.

short_title: Pipe to grep masks runner exit code; post-summary crash reads green
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' and the manifest's integrity grounding ('a green check must never mask it'; PASS certified against non-real state, cf. #313): an engine SIGABRT after the Totals printed was reported and re-verified downstream as green because the pipeline returned grep's exit status — the verification record disagreed with reality with zero factory-side signal; the proposed pipefail/exit-code-capture templates close a systematic false-green class across every pipe-into-grep verification convention.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true — never quick-win-eligible; the false-green verification hole gates functional verdicts and the fix spans role prompts/skills templates plus crash-forensics correlation, not a single mechanical edit.

short_title: Overlapping attestation controls make pixels contradict code
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'tightens an existing gate' under the self-referential inversion: a real driver scene stacked two buttons at one origin so every screenshot showed the focus ring on the wrong control, forcing the multimodal reviewer to flag a false contradiction; the proposed obligation (unique labels, non-overlapping placement, idle-state visibility gate before plan acceptance) hardens the attestation gate against both false findings and false blessings — the exact class visual attestation exists to prevent; cross-ref #636.

short_title: verify-git-push non-deterministically blocks non-force git push
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'closes a real defect class with a load-bearing test': pilot-derived reproduction of the highest grade — seven back-to-back trials on rc.22 including a byte-identical command string blocked once and passed once, ruling out syntactic matching and isolating hidden resolver state; names the latent fail-open direction (a stateful safety guard can silently stop protecting) and proposes a table-driven determinism test over compound-command forms.
quick_win_disqualification: Root cause unconfirmed — stale cache / shared mutable state / race in the resolver requires investigation and a concurrency-safe fix in a safety-critical guard; not bounded or low-caution.

short_title: Step guard counts STEP_COMPLETE per stop-event, forces re-emission
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'a study/observation request with no fix yet ... neutral-to-advances depending on whether it surfaces a real drift surface': pilot-derived (real PR delivery, merge independently verified against GitHub) but explicitly filed at observation grade — the per-stop-event counting root cause is the agent's inference awaiting maintainer confirmation, impact self-assessed LOW (redundant re-emission, token waste); the potential false 'steps missing' block on genuinely-finished work is the surfaced drift surface that keeps this from being noise.

short_title: Call-contract fix sweep misses godoc header and sibling BC specs
alignment_rationale: Rubric '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap ... that hardens the factory's own gates/convergence — on-mission': a real convergence run where a mutation-verified security fix's cascade was under-scoped twice — the function's godoc header (Miss 1, MED) and a second behavioral-contract doc still specifying the vulnerable expression (Miss 2, HIGH) — each miss costing a full extra fresh-context adversarial pass; the issue distinguishes itself concretely from #470/#564/#596 and asks to close the class one layer earlier.
quick_win_disqualification: cascade-sweep obligation is a dispatch-protocol change spanning implementer+orchestrator (enumerate-and-adjudicate checklist), not a bounded mechanical fix

short_title: New BC error code not registered in canonical error-taxonomy
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'tightens an existing gate / removes a drift surface': recurrence of a class the taxonomy's own audit history graded Finding 1 (BLOCKER) — same agent (product-owner), same BC, third orphan code — caught only by orchestrator disk-verification diligence; proposes a concrete mechanical validator (extract error-code tokens from BC tables, assert taxonomy rows) where today only agent diligence carries the obligation. Not integrity-flagged: the instance was caught in-burst and reconciled, so no open unsignaled corruption — the report is the missing guard.

short_title: Convergence counts clean passes without vector-coverage novelty
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'improves traceability or convergence soundness' plus the manifest's integrity anchor 'the ratchet / convergence gate definition': BC-5.39.001 as written counts finding-absence with no coverage-novelty floor, so the gate can certify 'clean' while defects live in unprobed vectors — invisible at the counter level. Evidence: akey STORY-024 pass ledger where passes 3–4 were code-clean (doc-only LOWs) while two MEDIUM signing-path defects (DI-004 silent audit-drop race, EC-005/6 dispatch violation) survived until pass 5's self-initiated new interleaving; both acceptance tests remove_file() before racing, funneling every prior look into one branch.
quick_win_disqualification: integrity=true categorically excludes quick-win; remedy requires convergence-gate redesign (per-pass vector-coverage tracking / coverage-aware dispatch), not a bounded mechanical change

attn.reason: External empirical evidence the maintainer cannot re-derive: a pass-by-pass finding trajectory from a private ArcavenAE pilot (akey STORY-024) demonstrating a concrete near-miss where the gate would have declared convergence over two live MEDIUM fail-closed-path defects; filer offers the full ledger on request.

short_title: Self-flagged follow-on sweep creates no tracked obligation
alignment_rationale: Rubric '(self-referential) a pilot-derived engine process-gap ... hardens the factory's own gates' / 'removes a drift surface': a quoted changelog deferral ('flagged for a follow-on sweep') produced no drift row, task, or machine-checkable marker; the sweep rotted across two subsequent revisions leaving stale GATED-style status markers in a governance artifact until a manual pre-delivery consistency pass forced it. Proposes the missing (c) option — tracked obligation instead of changelog prose — plus an automatable pre-delivery marker-vs-state scan.
quick_win_disqualification: requires designing a tracked-obligation mechanism in pipeline state and orchestrator dispatch practice; process design, not a bounded mechanical fix

short_title: Story index row accumulates unbounded per-revision changelog prose
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'removes a drift surface': measured evidence — one STORY-INDEX row at ~25,000 chars carrying 16 inlined per-version sub-narratives, index at ~315 KB / ~445 lines — duplicating each story's authoritative changelog into an always-scanned artifact (redundant storage that drifts, doubles maintenance surface) with no size budget or compaction path, the exact 'content migrates to the always-loaded document' failure mode STATE.md's line budget already guards against.

short_title: RED gate ignores predicted-vs-observed failure signature mismatch
alignment_rationale: Rubric 'tightens an existing gate': the RED gate verifies THAT tests fail, not that they fail as predicted. Observed instance: narration predicted two failing asserts, the RED run showed one (the engine shares the guard's IEEE blind spot — NAN <= 0 is false — and accepts the value silently); the mismatch was visible at commit time, nobody reconciled, the false empirical claim survived GREEN and review until a fresh-context revert-probe plus a controlled raw-engine probe refuted it, while the governing spec's rationale had said the opposite all along. Sibling of #607/#620/#652 with a distinct reconciliation remedy.
quick_win_disqualification: requires RED-gate protocol change (structured predicted-signature capture plus delivery-step predicted-vs-observed reconciliation), not a bounded mechanical fix
